title: Ensure nosuid option set on /var partition

prose.en: Ensure nosuid option set on /var partition

description.en: The `nosuid` mount option specifies that the filesystem cannot contain `setuid` files.

rationale.en: Since the `/var` filesystem is only intended for variable files such as logs, set this option to ensure that users cannot create `setuid` files in `/var`.

audit.en:
**\- IF \-** a separate partition exists for `/var`.  

Edit the `/etc/fstab` file and add `nosuid` to the fourth field (mounting options) for the `/var` partition.  

*Example:*  

Run the following command to remount `/var` with the configured options:

```
# mount -o remount /var
```

```
 /var         defaults,rw,nosuid,nodev,noexec,relatime  0 0
```

remediation.en:
**\- IF \-** a separate partition exists for `/var`.  

Edit the `/etc/fstab` file and add `nosuid` to the fourth field (mounting options) for the `/var` partition.  

*Example:*  

Run the following command to remount `/var` with the configured options:

```
# mount -o remount /var
```

```
 /var         defaults,rw,nosuid,nodev,noexec,relatime  0 0
```

references: See the fstab(5) manual page for more information.
